name: EmailPasswordLogin
description: Authenticates user with email + password. Returns JWT access token + refresh token, or HTTP error codes for 2FA/profile-selection flows.
steps: 1. Lookup user by email via UserService.GetApiUserByEmail()
2. Check lockout: if IsLockedOut=true OR (FailedLoginCount > 5 AND time-based delay not expired) → return HTTP 403
3. Hash submitted password with PasswordSalt using SHA256 → compare to stored Password
4. On match: reset FailedLoginCount=0, IsLockedOut=false, DateLastLoginUtc=now
5. If CurrentProfileId==0 AND user has exactly 1 profile → auto-set CurrentProfileId
6. If CurrentProfileId==0 AND user has multiple profiles → return HTTP 300 (client must show profile selector)
7. Create/extend RefreshToken (30 min), generate AccessToken JWT (15 min)
8. Return TokenDto
9. On mismatch: increment FailedLoginCount, update DateLastFailedLoginUtc → return HTTP 401
lockout_rule: FACT: FailedLoginCount > 5 AND (UtcNow - DateLastFailedLoginUtc).Minutes < (FailedLoginCount - 5) → locked. Auto-lockout is time-scaled (harder to unlock as count grows).

name: RefreshAccessToken
description: Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access token. Extends refresh token expiry on use.
steps: 1. GET /api/user/refreshaccesstoken?refreshToken=<token>
2. Lookup UserRefreshToken by token string (only non-expired)
3. If not found or expired → HTTP 401
4. Load User by token.UserId
5. Generate new AccessToken JWT (15 min)
6. ExtendRefreshToken (sliding window, 30 min from now)
7. Return new TokenDto (same RefreshTokenModel, new AccessToken)

name: Logout
description: Invalidates user session server-side. GAP_001 was wrong — logout IS implemented.
steps: 1. POST /api/user/logout (authorized)
2. Check _workContext.IsLoggedIn
3. Call _authenticationService.Logout(_workContext.CurrentUser.Id)
4. Return HTTP 200
note: Logout invalidates refresh token server-side. Access token remains valid until 15-min TTL expires (by design — short-lived JWT).

name: AzureADLogin (Entra ID / MSAL)
description: Azure AD / Entra ID login via MSAL. Extracts idToken from MSAL result and exchanges for ServiceAlert JWT.
steps: 1. MsalBroadcastService emits LOGIN_SUCCESS or ACQUIRE_TOKEN_SUCCESS
2. Extract idToken from AuthenticationResult
3. POST /api/user/loginad { token: idToken, smsGroupId? }
4. On success: store adLogin='1' in localStorage for silent SSO on next load
5. On HTTP 300: show profile selector (slideContainerValue=2)
6. On HTTP 428 + authenticatorApp=true: show TOTP input (slideContainerValue=5)
7. On HTTP 428 + authenticatorApp=false: send pin code by email
8. On subsequent loads: attempt ssoSilent() first; if fails, remove adLogin flag

name: TwoFactorAuthentication
description: 2FA flow with 3 delivery methods: SMS pin, email pin, or TOTP authenticator app.
methods: sms: pin sent to User.ResetPhone + User.PhoneCode
email: pin sent to User.Email via UserService.send2FaCodeByEmail()
authenticatorApp: user reads TOTP from app (no dispatch needed). Validated via ValidateAuthenticatorCode(code, User.AuthenticatorSecret, DateTime)
pin_storage: In-memory or short-lived (NOT in DB for SMS/email pins). Only AuthenticatorSecret stored on User.

name: PasswordReset
description: User requests password reset via email. Token (Guid) stored on User entity with expiry.
steps: 1. User submits reset email in slide state 3
2. POST /api/user/passwordresetrequest → generates Guid token, sets User.PasswordResetToken + User.DatePasswordResetTokenExpiresUtc
3. Email sent with link containing token
4. User clicks link → GET /new-password?token=<guid>
5. On submit: POST /api/user/newpassword → validates token + expiry, calls SetNewPassword()
6. SetNewPassword(): validates password rules via IsPasswordValid(), generates new salt + hash
security_note: PasswordResetToken is Guid — predictable length. TD: use 256-bit CryptoRandom instead.

name: UserNudging
description: System prompts users with contextual suggestions. Users can dismiss permanently (neverAgain=true) or postpone.
nudge_types: AuthenticatorApp — prompts user to set up 2FA authenticator app (identity_access domain)
MessageSendToOwner — prompts to send message to property owners (messaging domain)
Eboks — prompts to enable e-Boks digital post (messaging domain)
Voice — prompts to enable voice calls (messaging domain)
api: GET nudging/blocks?smsGroupId → UserNudgingBlocksDto (map of NudgeType → bool blocked)
PUT nudging/response → SaveUserNudgingResponseCommand { nudgeType, neverAgain, response, smsGroupId }
local_storage: surveyNudgePostponeDateTimeUtc stored in localStorage for 1-hour postpone

name: Impersonation
description: Admin can impersonate another user. Original user identity preserved in JWT claim.
jwt_claim: impersonateFromUserId claim added to access token when impersonating
entity_field: User.ImpersonatingUserId (int?, nullable) — also stored on User entity
note: INFERRED: Super admin feature. Original user session must be restored on exit.

name: SCIMProvisioning
description: SCIM 2.0 inbound user/group provisioning from external IdPs (e.g., Azure AD, Okta).

name: GetCurrentUser
description: Returns current user data from WorkContext. Two endpoints: full UserDto (GET /user) and lightweight UserInfoDto (GET /user/info).
access_control: CanUserAccessUser(currentUserId, requestedId) — users can only access themselves or users they manage

description: User navigates to /sso/{customerId}. System reads CustomerSamlSettings (EntityId, MetadataUrl, EmailClaim). Redirects to customer IdP. IdP returns SAML assertion. System extracts email from claim named in EmailClaim (often NameID for Azure AD). Looks up User by email. Completes login and issues JWT+RefreshToken.
name: SamlSSOLogin
rules: Users MUST navigate via /sso/{customerId} — cannot bookmark direct login page
EmailClaim field = name of SAML claim containing email, found by testing with actual IdP login
SustainSys.Saml2 library used. Metadata published at /Saml2
SAML cert stored in Azure Key Vault, loaded via WEBSITE_LOAD_CERTIFICATES + Saml2:CertificateThumbprint app settings

description: Annual renewal (cert expires Oct 19). Process: verify DNS validation → manual renew in cert portal → export public key → notify customer → upload new cert to App Service → update thumbprint on staging slot → swap deployment slots.
name: SamlCertRenewal
rules: ONLY send public key to customers (never private key)
Cert swap via deployment slot swap (not manual override)
Staging slot holds next cert; production slot holds current
IdP systems support overlapping certs — safe to distribute new cert before swap

description: Azure AD customers require admin consent granted once per organization in Azure AD portal → Enterprise Applications → SMS-Service Azure AD Login → Permissions → Grant admin consent.
name: AzureADSSO
rules: Without admin consent: each user sees consent popup on every login
Admin consent is a one-time per-tenant operation performed by customer IT admin

description: SLO (Single Logout): user logs out at IdP → IdP sends SLO request to /Saml2 handler → refresh token invalidated → user redirected to other SPs for SLO → final logout complete. KNOWN RISK: access token remains valid in browser for up to 15min after SLO (no server-side JWT revocation).
name: SingleLogout
rules: SLO is supported by SustainSys library
Access token expiry is the only bound on post-SLO window
Consider browser-side token destruction for complete SLO implementation

description: User clicks 'Glemt adgangskode?' → enters email → system sends password reset email. User sets new password meeting requirements: min 8 chars, at least one digit, one lowercase, one uppercase, one special char from set: *$-+?_&=!%/{}()#
name: PasswordReset
rules: Password min 8 chars
Must contain: 1 digit, 1 lowercase, 1 uppercase, 1 special from: *$-+?_&=!%/{}()#
'Send email for new password' button also available from admin user edit page
Reset email may land in spam/junk folder — documented in user guide

description: System monitors whether SSO sign-in is operational. SSO availability check runs periodically. Monitoring preserves exception details for easier debugging if SSO login is unavailable.
name: SSOMonitoring

trigger: User with CanSendByLocationAlert selects LocationAlert as send method in ServiceAlert UI.
name: LocationAlertGetSessionTicket
description: When user with CanSendByLocationAlert ProfileRole launches LocationAlert from ServiceAlert, ServiceAlert creates a LocationAlertSession row with a one-time TicketId (GUID, expires 1 minute). Returns the TicketId to the UI. UI redirects user to LocationAlert with ticket as query parameter + '&site=SA'. Creating a ticket INVALIDATES all existing LocationAlertSessions for the UserId.

LocationAlert calls ServiceAlert with the ticket received from browser redirect. ServiceAlert validates ticket (not expired, not redeemed, exists). Returns a SessionId (GUID). Records DateTicketRedeemedUtc. The SessionId is used by LocationAlert for all subsequent calls.
auth: Header: X-V24-Service: Lokasjonsvarsling (anonymous to ServiceAlert users)
response_failure: 404 Not Found (ticket expired, already redeemed, or doesn't exist)
name: LocationAlertRedeemTicket
rules: Ticket TTL = 1 minute
Ticket is single-use — DateTicketRedeemedUtc set on redeem
Same 404 response for expired/redeemed/missing — no oracle attack surface

name: LocationAlertDirectLogin
rules: Session created without ticket when user logs in via LocationAlert credentials
Lockout: 423 Locked (not 403 like normal login)
mfa_path: If MFA: blank SessionId returned → user enters PIN → /LocationAlert/verifypincode
description: LocationAlert passes user credentials directly to ServiceAlert for validation. If no MFA: returns SessionId immediately. If MFA required: sends PIN to user's phone, returns blank SessionId (LocationAlert prompts for PIN). On failure: 401 Unauthorized. On lockout: 423 Locked. Creates LocationAlertSession WITHOUT a TicketId.

name: LocationAlertVerifyPinCode
description: Confirms 2FA PIN for LocationAlert MFA flow. On success returns SessionId. Session is now fully active.

integration_note: Response shape is fixed to match Varsling24 API contract — cannot be changed without coordinating with LocationAlert team.
municipality_source: Municipality positive list configured for the customer. First = main/Hoved.
name: LocationAlertGetUserProfile
description: Returns profile information in Varsling24 format. Firmanavn (customer name), Orgnr (company reg), Kommunenr/navn (municipality from positive list — first = hoved, all listed). SmsAvsender (SmsSendAs). Bruker (userId, full name, email, mobile, Admin=SuperAdmin flag, Simulering=AlwaysTestMode, Tilgang.LokasjonsVarsling=CanSendByLocationAlert).

rules: Session must be renewed — not just validated
440 status code is non-standard (telnet legacy) — required by LocationAlert
Check CanSendByLocationAlert on each heartbeat — if role removed, session becomes invalid
name: LocationAlertIsSessionActive
description: Periodic heartbeat from LocationAlert. Validates: session exists, not expired, user still has CanSendByLocationAlert. If valid: renews DateSessionExpiresUtc to NOW()+8 hours. Returns 200 with SessionId. If invalid: 440 Login Time-out with blank SessionId.

single_sign_out: RedirectUrl in response enables LocationAlert frontend to redirect user to ServiceAlert /logout, where ServiceAlert refresh token is also revoked.
name: LocationAlertLogout
description: Invalidates the LocationAlertSession. If session had a TicketId (user came via ServiceAlert SSO): response includes RedirectUrl pointing to ServiceAlert logout page, allowing single-sign-out. If session was direct login (no TicketId): returns empty JSON.
